channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Supreme Court upholds a Biden-era rule regulating ghost guns
item.description: The court says ghost guns, which are virtually untraceable weapons that require assembly, are considered "firearms" and subject to ATF regulation.
item.dc:creator: NPR Washington Desk, MPR News

item.title: Most US Institute of Peace workers get late-night word of their mass firing
item.description: Most employees at the U.S. Institute of Peace, a congressionally created and funded think tank now taken over by Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency, have received email notices of their mass firing — the latest step in the Trump administration’s government downsizing.
item.dc:creator: Gary Fields And Matthew Lee, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: American woman held in Afghanistan by the Taliban has been released, AP source says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An American woman detained for weeks in Afghanistan by the Taliban has been released from custody, according to a person familiar with the matter and a social media post Saturday from a longtime U.S. diplomat.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Richard Carlbom ascends to top role at Minnesota DFL Party as its first new chair in 14 years
item.description: Richard Carlbom was chosen as the Minnesota DFL Party chair, rising to the top post after years of working behind the scenes on party elections and causes.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Trump appoints Sara Carter as next drug czar, says she 'will lead the charge to protect' US
item.description: President Donald Trump appointed Sara Carter as drug czar, saying she will "lead the charge to protect our nation and save our children from the scourge of drugs.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Meet the DOGE team
item.description: Several members of Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) made their first public appearance. Here's who they are.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, NewsNation

item.title: Republican lawmaker confronted over Signal chat leak, responds sharply
item.description: Rep. Tim Burchett, R-Tenn., had a harsh response to a man asking him about his trust in the Trump team after the Signal chat leak. The congressman's reply seemed to stop the man in his tracks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump increasingly asks the Supreme Court to overrule judges blocking key parts of his agenda
item.description: President Donald Trump's administration has increasingly turned to the use of emergency appeals to the Supreme Court as it faces more than 130 lawsuits over his executive orders.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sherman And Lindsay Whitehurst, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump draws laughs when defining a 'woman' — until he touches on a serious issue
item.description: When asked to define a "woman," President Donald Trump started with funny lines before turning to a serious issue, the inclusion of transgender athletes in women's sports, which he called "crazy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Illinois to defy Trump voter order for April election
item.description: President Trump earlier this week issued an executive order calling for all voters to provide proof of citizenship before receiving a ballot.
item.dc:creator: BJ Lutz, WGN 9

item.title: Trump admin’s first oil and gas-lease sales nets nearly $40 million for U.S. coffers
item.description: Interior Secretary Doug Burgum lauded millions in federal windfall from sales of oil and gas leases in Trump's first three months.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: State Republicans say Sigal Chattah nominated for Nevada U.S. Attorney post
item.description: Nevada Republicans are saying Sigal Chattah has been nominated to serve as interim U.S. Attorney overseeing Nevada.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Hegseth’s younger brother is serving in a key role as liaison and senior adviser inside the Pentagon
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth’s younger brother is serving in a key position inside the Pentagon as a Department of Homeland Security liaison and senior adviser, Hegseth's office confirmed.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judges extend orders against deportation flights, including wartime act on violent Venezuelan gang members
item.description: A federal judge ruled Friday that a restraining order on the Alien Enemies Act will be extended through April 12, and another judge in Boston blocked the deportation of migrants.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump doubles down on acquiring Greenland for ‘world peace’ amid Vance visit
item.description: President Donald Trump has repeatedly offered, dating back to his first administration, a desire to secure Greenland for the U.S. as Russian and Chinese presence grows in the Arctic.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Elon Musk to hand out $1M checks ahead of judicial race in Wisconsin
item.description: Elon Musk said he would be traveling to Wisconsin before the Supreme Court race to speak with voters and give out $1 million checks to several people who have already cast ballots.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, NewsNation

item.title: Texas hospital treating children with vitamin A toxicity: Reports
item.description: The CDC notes that it is not a substitute for vaccination, and warns that overusing Vitamin A can lead to toxicity.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: McConnell: US headed for ‘Russia wins, America loses’ headline on Ukraine deal
item.description: Former Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell (Ky.) warned in a speech Thursday that U.S. negotiators trying to end the war in Ukraine are headed for a “Russia wins, America loses” headline if they agree to a deal that achieves only “illusory peace.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota lawyers say increased immigration enforcement is filling courtrooms
item.description: It’s been just more than two months since the Trump administration began implementing its plans to deport more people in the country without legal status. And the impact is being felt at the Fort Snelling Immigration Court.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Thamer, MPR News

item.title: Trump tells automakers not to raise prices over tariff: Official
item.description: President Donald Trump believes costs associated with the auto tariff should not be passed onto the American people
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Kentucky Gov. Andy Beshear displays his colors in veto fights with Republican lawmakers
item.description: FRANKFORT, Ky. (AP) — Knowing the odds of winning were stacked against him, Democratic Gov. Andy Beshear nevertheless vetoed bills sent his way by the Republican-dominated Kentucky legislature with by-now familiar themes: abortion, Medicaid and diversity initiatives.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Noted economist honored by Trump warns his 25% tariffs could add $4,711 to the cost of a vehicle
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Noted economist Arthur Laffer warns in a new analysis that President Donald Trump's 25% tariffs on auto imports could add $4,711 to the cost of a vehicle and says the proposed taxes could weaken the ability of U.S. automakers to compete with their foreign counterparts.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump: House should take up Senate-passed DC funding fix immediately
item.description: President Trump is urging swift passage of legislation to fix what members have called a “mistake” by Congress that forced significant cuts to Washington, D.C.’s local budget. Trump on Friday wrote on Truth Social that the House should “immediately” take up the “D.C.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Mark Kelly hits back at Musk: ‘Elon standing with' billionaires
item.description: Sen. Mark Kelly (D-Ariz.) slammed what he called senior Trump adviser Elon Musk’s loyalties to billionaires Thursday after Musk accused the Arizona senator and decorated combat veteran of putting other countries ahead of the United States, the latest salvo in their rancorous feud.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minnesota doctor: Hospitals in ‘existential crisis’ and potential Medicaid cuts won’t help
item.description: Dr. Rahul Koranne, president and CEO of the Minnesota Hospital Association, joined Cathy Wurzer to talk about what recent job loss and federal volatility mean for the health care of all Minnesotans.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Vance accuses Denmark of underinvesting in Greenland as Trump presses for US takeover of the island
item.description: U.S. Vice President JD Vance says Denmark has “underinvested” in Greenland’s security and demands Denmark change its approach as President Donald Trump continues to talk of taking over the Danish territory.
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Grieshaber, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Per-pupil funding going up $2 next year unless something changes in Nevada budget
item.description: Republican Gov. Joe Lombardo's proposed state budget sets per-pupil funding in 2026 at $9,416, just $2 higher than the current funding level. Going higher than that is "impracticable.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Battle over proxy voting for new parents intensifies in House GOP
item.description: The battle between Rep. Anna Paulina Luna (R-Fla.) and House GOP leaders over looming floor action to allow proxy voting for new parents is intensifying as both sides of the heated debate dig in their heels — with the clash potentially coming to a head as soon as next week.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Congress questions the FAA, US Army and NTSB over deadly midair collision in DC
item.description: The head of the National Transportation Safety Board appeared alongside officials from the Federal Aviation Administration and the Army's aviation division at a hearing on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Josh Funk, Fox 5

item.title: Turkish student at Tufts University detained, video shows masked people handcuffing her
item.description: A lawyer for a Turkish national and doctoral student at Tufts University says she has been detained by Department of Homeland Security agents without explanation.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Is the fight over? Florida bill to allow guns on campus gets shot down
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (WFLA) — A rare rejection happened at the state capitol as a Republican-led bill was voted down by a fellow GOP lawmaker. Senate Bill 814 would have allowed concealed carry on college campuses, but it died in its first committee stop.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: What we know about Minnesota’s government employee return to office plan
item.description: Gov. Tim Walz has directed workers to report to offices or job sites at least 50 percent of the time beginning in June. Employee unions are pushing back.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, Clay Masters, and Peter Cox, MPR News

item.title: 5 takeaways from House hearing on NPR, PBS
item.description: The heads of the nation's top public broadcasters faced an intense grilling from lawmakers Wednesday over their outlets’ funding and editorial objectivity.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, Fox 5

item.title: NYC mayoral poll: Cuomo grows lead, Mamdani gains traction
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Former Gov. Andrew Cuomo is still holding onto the lead in the New York City mayoral race – but one other candidate is also gaining support, according to new exclusive polling conducted by PIX11 News in partnership with Emerson College and The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Grace Gomez, PIX 11

item.title: Trump portrait to be removed from Colorado Capitol after president's criticism
item.description: President Trump has criticized a portrait of himself hanging in the Colorado Capitol, suggesting it was intentionally distorted, while Republican lawmakers have asked for it to be removed.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, Fox 5

item.title: Judge won't lift block on Trump use of Alien Enemies Act to deport Venezuelans
item.description: U.S. District Court Judge James Boasberg declined to lift a restraining order barring the Trump administration from using the Alien Enemies Act to deport Venezuelans, determining migrants are likely to succeed in pushing for hearings on whether they are in a gang.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5
